game_name: Romance of the Three Kingdoms III: Dragon of Destiny

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players choose from 6 historical scenarios (AD 189-235) that establish initial power distribution and warlord territories
Players select which warlord(s) to control; custom characters can be inserted into unoccupied territories
Each character has 6 stats (War Ability, Intelligence, Politics, Charisma, Army Command, Naval Command) affecting gameplay decisions
Control of 46 cities on the map; victory requires conquering all territories across ancient China
Hundreds of unique characters available for recruitment and strategic deployment across controlled cities
Naval Command stat becomes relevant in scenarios involving water-based territorial control and expansion

typical_relief_triggers: Strategic pause between turns for planning
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turn-based strategic pacing allows player-controlled tension management; long-term campaign rhythm spans multiple in-game years per scenario

difficulty_curve.early_game: Reviewers noted this entry geared towards series/genre fans; easier than predecessor Romance of the Three Kingdoms II; scenario selection allows difficulty modulation
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Strategic complexity increases as multiple warlord factions compete for territories; character stat management becomes critical
difficulty_curve.late_game: Endgame focuses on territorial consolidation across all 46 cities; naval command becomes increasingly relevant
notable_spikes: Transition from early territorial control to managing multiple simultaneous frontier expansions
Competition for high-stat character recruitment in mid-game population pools

notable_level_patterns.description: Six distinct starting scenarios (plus one in PC-Engine CD version) representing different periods of the Three Kingdoms era; each establishes unique initial territorial distributions and warlord allegiances based on novel but diverging from historical narrative after game start
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Dong Zhuo's reign of terror (AD 189) - earliest scenario; Emergence of the Crouching Dragon (AD 208) - mid-point scenario

notable_level_patterns.description: Single unified map with 46 cities; player victory defined by capturing all territories through strategic deployment of recruited characters and armies; no linear progression structure
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All scenarios share this pattern; map does not change structure between scenarios

notable_level_patterns.description: Hundreds of unique historical characters with individual stat distributions; player progression determined by recruitment strategy and character stat combinations rather than level-based advancement
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Jiang Wei inherits Kongming's legacy (AD 235) - endgame scenario with established factions requiring specialized character recruitment strategies
